details: Hang at Phase 11 add<cb_sum_old,cb_sum_new,cb_sum_old>. Reading chain impl to understand lifecycle order.
Copy Q to output passes. Reader/descriptor/CB setup is correct. Issue is in compute phases.

Two issues: (1) CT arg mismatch from linter, (2) Phase 5 eltwise_chain with Block operand kind does not produce cb_alpha output. DEVICE_PRINT confirms alp=0 after P5, sno=0 at P7, leading to P11 hang.
Symptom: TRISC0 stuck at add waiting for cb_sum_new after SUM reduce. SUM+REDUCE_ROW uses matmul path which skips reduce_uninit cleanup. Reproducing hang.
Reading all kernel files + descriptor. Implementer breadcrumbs show NCRISC never started (waiting for BRISC notification). Investigating RISC launch order and config.
Reading all kernel files and design doc. Tracing CB lifecycle for shape (1,1,32,32): B_q_t=1, B_kv_t=1, D_t=1, S_q_tiles=1, S_kv_tiles=1

Tracing cb_scaler_reduce (2 pages) for shape (1,1,32,32): reader pushes MAX scaler then SUM scaler (2 total). Compute: MAX reduce internally waits 1 scaler, then compute kernel manually waits+pops 1. Then SUM reduce internally waits 1, then compute kernel manually waits+pops 1. Total pops=2, pushes=2. Balanced.

description: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: matmul shape hardcoded to of(2,2,2,2,D_t,1) which expects M=4,N=4. But for test shape (1,1,32,32): B_q_t=1,B_kv_t=1,D_t=1. Reader only pushes 1 Q tile and 1 K tile. Matmul waits for in0_block_num_tiles=4 and in1_block_num_tiles=4 tiles. Deadlock at QK^T matmul wait_front. Fix: derive matmul shape from B_q_t and B_kv_t, use 1x1 subblocks when block size < 2.

details: Fresh triage shows: NCRISC (reader) stuck at wait_for_brisc_notification (ncrisc.cc:128) - never launched. BRISC (writer) at cb_wait_front(cb_out). TRISC0 at matmul_block wait_front (Phase 1 QK^T). Root cause: reader never starts, so no data pushed to CBs, compute hangs waiting for Q/K tiles.

description: NCRISC at wait_for_brisc_notification means reader has ALREADY run and finished (returned to firmware loop). BRISC passed start_ncrisc_kernel_run before writer kernel. Reader may have exited without pushing all data. Need DEVICE_PRINT to trace reader pushes and compute CB state at each phase.

Linter has completely rewritten all 3 kernels + descriptor. New version uses FillScalar for init (compute-side), separate scaler CBs (6,7), InputLifecycle::Bulk for Phase 5, in-place add for Phase 11. Simulator compile fails with div-by-zero at matmul_block instantiation. Hardware uses stale cache from old kernel version. Cannot get reliable test results due to device contention + linter overwrites.

details: Multi-tile hang (128x128): Phase 5 eltwise_chain uses OperandKind::Scalar for cb_max_old with InputLifecycle::Bulk. Scalar window=1, so pop_upfront_end only pops 1 tile from cb_max_old. But init pushes B_q_t=4 tiles. After Phase 5, 3 tiles remain. Phase 13 copy<cb_max_new,cb_max_old> tries to write 4 tiles but only 1 slot free -> reserve_back deadlock. Fix: change OperandKind::Scalar to OperandKind::Block for cb_max_old and cb_max_new in Phase 5.

Root cause identified for multi-tile hang: OperandKind::Scalar in Phase 5 alpha computation only pops 1 tile from cb_max_old (via Bulk pop_upfront_end), but B_q_t tiles were pushed. Fix: OperandKind::Block pops Ht*Wt=B_q_t tiles. Implementer applied fix in commit 23736628be. Secondary issue: tensix_sync in pack_reconfig_data_format can deadlock with mop_sync in math hoist_compute_init when transitioning from matmul_block to eltwise chain.

Test passes in both --dev and non-dev modes. Background agent (ttnn-implementer) resolved the hang by: (1) splitting scaler CBs into cb_scaler_max=6 and cb_scaler_sum=7, (2) using FillScalar for init state in compute kernel, (3) fixing CT arg alignment, (4) using OperandKind::Block with InputLifecycle::Bulk for phase 5 alpha
Numerical mismatch in flash attention. PCC 0.94 on multi-KV-block shapes. All-0.5 input passes. num_kv_blocks=1 always passes. Starting investigation with shape (1,1,128,256).
Error is tile-row pattern: tile 0 of Q-block (rows 0-31) correct, tiles 1-3 (rows 32-127) wrong. num_kv_blocks=2. Suggests multi-tile Q-block handling bug in broadcast or indexing.

Alpha values in KVB=1 are WRONG at element [0,16]. Device shows alpha[0]=[0,0]=1.839 [0,16]=1.0, but ref shows [0,0]=1.844 [0,16]=1.844 (should be same - per-row value). The alpha tile has col 0 correct but col 16 = 1.0 (default/uninitialized?).

REDUCE_ROW output tiles have correct value at column 0 but 0 at all other columns. max_new[0] device=[2.375, 0] but should be [2.375, 2.375] (replicated across all 32 cols). This causes alpha=exp(0-0)=1 at non-zero columns instead of the correct value.

recip_l values are wrong for tile rows 1-3. tile[0] matches ref but tiles 1-3 have wrong l_i. Column 16 of all tiles = 0 (expected for reduce output). The column-0 values themselves are wrong for tiles 1-3.

Phase 7 l*=alpha produces wrong values for tiles 1,3 but correct for 0,2. Alpha column-0 values look correct. Need to check if the mul_tiles_bcast<COL> is reading the correct alpha tile or if the sum_old tiles are in wrong order.

Phase 7 mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old, BroadcastDim::Col, ..., OperandKind::Scalar, OperandKind::Col>(B_q_t) passes B_q_t as bare uint32_t, creating EltwiseShape(Ht=1, Wt=4). With OperandKind::Col, idx returns ht=0 for all iterations, so alpha[0] is used for ALL tiles instead of alpha[ht]. Fix: use EltwiseShape::col(B_q_t) to create Ht=B_q_t, Wt=1.

Fix verified: PCC 0.999997 on debug test (1,1,128,64)x(1,1,256,64). Acceptance test: 30/34 pass (up from 26/34). 4 remaining failures are all custom_mask variants with num_kv_blocks=1 — separate pre-existing issue unrelated to the alpha bug.
